sanskrit:
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरुनन्दन।
बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम्॥
transliteration: vyavasāyātmikā buddhir ekeha kuru-nandana | bahu-śākhā hy anantāś ca buddhayo ’vyavasāyinām ||
literal_meaning: A single-pointed, resolute intelligence exists here, O joy of the Kurus; the intelligences of the irresolute are many-branched and endless.
contextual_meaning: The mind that commits to one overarching value (Kṛṣṇa-consciousness) becomes unified and decisive; scattered goals create infinite inner committees, each pulling the ego in a different direction.
psychological_state: Decision-paralysis produced by competing internal objects (roles, duties, desires); ego lacks a super-ordinate narrative to hierarchize choices.
trigger_scenario: A mid-career professional is offered three simultaneous promotions in different cities, each with ethical trade-offs; sleepless nights, pros-and-cons spreadsheets, and conflicting advice from family, mentors, and social media leave them frozen.
anchor_text: Pick one guiding purpose; let every choice serve it alone.
